<s>[INST]Code:import heapq

class Solution:
    def minimumEffortPath(self, heights):
        first, last = 0, 1000000 - 1
        answer = last
        while first < last:
            middle = (first + last) // 2
            if self.possible(heights, middle):
                answer = min(answer, middle)
                last = middle
            else:
                first = middle + 1
        return answer

    def possible(self, heights, search):
        m, n = len(heights), len(heights[0])
        directions = [(0, 1), (1, 0), (0, -1), (-1, 0)]
        pq = [(0, 0, 0)]  # (effort, x, y)
        visited = [[False] * n for _ in range(m)]
        visited[0][0] = True

        while pq:
            metric = heapq.heappop(pq)
            effort, x, y = metric
            if x == m - 1 and y == n - 1:
                return True
            for dx, dy in directions:
                nx, ny = x + dx, y + dy
                if 0 <= nx < m and 0 <= ny < n and not visited[nx][ny]:
                    new_cost = abs(heights[nx][ny] - heights[x][y])
                    if new_cost <= search:
                        heapq.heappush(pq, (max(new_cost, effort), nx, ny))
                        visited[nx][ny] = True
        return False

BEGIN FUNCTION minimumEffortPath WITH INPUT heights
    SET first TO 0
    SET last TO 1000000 MINUS 1
    SET answer TO last
    WHILE first LESS THAN last DO
        SET middle TO (first PLUS last) DIVIDED BY 2
        IF CALL FUNCTION possible WITH INPUT heights, middle IS TRUE
            SET answer TO MINIMUM OF answer, middle
            SET last TO middle
        ELSE
            SET first TO middle PLUS 1
        END IF
    END WHILE
    RETURN answer
END FUNCTION

BEGIN FUNCTION possible WITH INPUT heights, search
    SET m TO length of heights
    SET n TO length of element AT 0 of heights
    INITIALIZE directions AS collection containing (0, 1), (1, 0), (0, MINUS 1), (MINUS 1, 0)
    INITIALIZE pq AS collection containing (0, 0, 0)
    INITIALIZE visited AS two-dimensional collection with m rows and n columns filled with FALSE
    SET element AT 0 of element AT 0 of visited TO TRUE
    WHILE pq IS NOT EQUAL TO EMPTY DO
        SET metric TO remove and return minimum element from pq
        SET effort TO first value of metric
        SET x TO second value of metric
        SET y TO third value of metric
        IF x EQUALS m MINUS 1 AND y EQUALS n MINUS 1
            RETURN TRUE
        END IF
        FOR EACH direction IN directions DO
            SET dx TO first value of direction
            SET dy TO second value of direction
            SET nx TO x PLUS dx
            SET ny TO y PLUS dy
            IF nx GREATER THAN OR EQUAL TO 0 AND nx LESS THAN m AND ny GREATER THAN OR EQUAL TO 0 AND ny LESS THAN n AND element AT ny of element AT nx of visited IS FALSE
                SET new_cost TO ABSOLUTE VALUE OF (element AT ny of element AT nx of heights MINUS element AT y of element AT x of heights)
                IF new_cost LESS THAN OR EQUAL TO search
                    PUSH (MAXIMUM OF new_cost, effort), nx, ny into minimum-priority pq WITH INPUT NULL
                    SET element AT ny of element AT nx of visited TO TRUE
                END IF
            END IF
        END FOR
    END WHILE
    RETURN FALSE
END FUNCTION

import heapq

class Solution:
    def minimumObstacles(self, grid: List[List[int]]) -> int:
        pq=[(0,0,0)]
        visited=set()
        m=len(grid)
        n=len(grid[0])

        cost_matrix = [[float('inf')] * n for _ in range(m)]
        cost_matrix[0][0]=0

        directions=[(1,0),(0,1),(-1,0),(0,-1)]

        while(pq):
            cost,x,y=heapq.heappop(pq)
            if (x,y) in visited:
                continue

            visited.add((x,y))

            if x==m-1 and y==n-1:
                return cost
            for dx,dy in directions:
                if(0<=x+dx<=m-1 and 0<=y+dy<=n-1):
                    new_cost=cost+grid[x+dx][y+dy]
                    if(new_cost<cost_matrix[x+dx][y+dy]):
                        heapq.heappush(pq,(cost+grid[x+dx][y+dy],x+dx,y+dy))
                        cost_matrix[x+dx][y+dy]=new_cost

        return -1

BEGIN FUNCTION minimumObstacles WITH INPUT grid
    INITIALIZE pq AS priority queue containing tuple of 0, 0, 0
    INITIALIZE visited AS empty set
    SET m TO length of grid
    SET n TO length of element AT 0 in grid
    INITIALIZE cost_matrix AS two-dimensional collection of size m by n filled with NULL
    FOR EACH row IN cost_matrix DO
        FOR EACH cell IN row DO
            SET cell TO very large value
        END FOR
    END FOR
    SET element AT 0 in element AT 0 in cost_matrix TO 0
    INITIALIZE directions AS collection containing tuples of 1, 0 and 0, 1 and MINUS 1, 0 and 0, MINUS 1
    WHILE pq IS NOT EQUAL TO EMPTY DO
        SET current TO remove and return minimum element from pq
        SET cost TO first value of current
        SET x TO second value of current
        SET y TO third value of current
        IF tuple of x, y exists in visited IS TRUE THEN CONTINUE
        ELSE
            ADD tuple of x,y TO visited
            IF x EQUALS m MINUS 1 AND y EQUALS n MINUS 1 THEN
                RETURN cost
            ELSE
                FOR EACH direction IN directions DO
                    SET dx TO first value of direction
                    SET dy TO second value of direction
                    IF x PLUS dx GREATER THAN OR EQUAL TO 0 AND x PLUS dx LESS THAN OR EQUAL TO m MINUS 1 AND y PLUS dy GREATER THAN OR EQUAL TO 0 AND y PLUS dy LESS THAN OR EQUAL TO n MINUS 1 THEN
                        SET new_cost TO cost PLUS element AT y PLUS dy in element AT x PLUS dx in grid
                        IF new_cost LESS THAN element AT y PLUS dy in element AT x PLUS dx in cost_matrix THEN
                            PUSH tuple of cost PLUS element AT y PLUS dy in element AT x PLUS dx in grid, x PLUS dx, y PLUS dy into pq
                            SET element AT y PLUS dy in element AT x PLUS dx in cost_matrix TO new_cost
                        ELSE
                        END IF
                    ELSE
                    END IF
                END FOR
            END IF
        END IF
    END WHILE
    RETURN MINUS 1
END FUNCTION

def partition(arr, low, high):
    pivot = arr[high]
    i = low - 1

    for j in range(low, high):
        if arr[j] <= pivot:
            i += 1
            arr[i], arr[j] = swap(arr[i], arr[j])

    arr[i + 1], arr[high] = swap(arr[i + 1], arr[high])
    return i + 1

def quick_sort(arr, low, high):
    if low < high:
        pi = partition(arr, low, high)
        quick_sort(arr, low, pi - 1)
        quick_sort(arr, pi + 1, high)

def print_array(arr):
    for i in arr:
        print(i, end=" ")
    print()

<s>[INST]Code:class Solution:
    def shortestCommonSupersequence(self, str1, str2):
        n, m = len(str1), len(str2)
        dp = [[0] * (n + 1) for _ in range(m + 1)]
        
        for i in range(m - 1, -1, -1):
            for j in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
                if str1[j] == str2[i]:
                    dp[i][j] = 1 + dp[i + 1][j + 1]
                else:
                    dp[i][j] = max(dp[i + 1][j], dp[i][j + 1])
        
        answer = []
        i, j = 0, 0
        
        while i < m and j < n:
            if str1[j] == str2[i]:
                answer.append(str2[i])
                i += 1
                j += 1
            elif dp[i + 1][j] > dp[i][j + 1]:
                answer.append(str2[i])
                i += 1
            else:
                answer.append(str1[j])
                j += 1
        
        while j < n:
            answer.append(str1[j])
            j += 1
        
        while i < m:
            answer.append(str2[i])
            i += 1
        
        return ''.join(answer)

BEGIN FUNCTION shortestCommonSupersequence WITH INPUT str1, str2
    SET n TO length of str1
    SET m TO length of str2
    INITIALIZE dp AS two-dimensional collection with m PLUS 1 rows and n PLUS 1 columns filled with 0
    FOR EACH i IN values from m MINUS 1 down to 0 DO
        FOR EACH j IN values from n MINUS 1 down to 0 DO
            IF element AT j of str1 EQUALS element AT i of str2 THEN
                SET element AT j of element AT i of dp TO 1 PLUS element AT j PLUS 1 of element AT i PLUS 1 of dp
            ELSE
                SET element AT j of element AT i of dp TO MAXIMUM OF element AT j of element AT i PLUS 1 of dp and element AT j PLUS 1 of element AT i of dp
            END IF
        END FOR
    END FOR
    INITIALIZE answer AS EMPTY
    SET i TO 0
    SET j TO 0
    WHILE i LESS THAN m AND j LESS THAN n DO
        IF element AT j of str1 EQUALS element AT i of str2 THEN
            APPEND element AT i of str2 TO answer
            SET i TO i PLUS 1
            SET j TO j PLUS 1
        ELSE
            IF element AT j of element AT i PLUS 1 of dp GREATER THAN element AT j PLUS 1 of element AT i of dp THEN
                APPEND element AT i of str2 TO answer
                SET i TO i PLUS 1
            ELSE
                APPEND element AT j of str1 TO answer
                SET j TO j PLUS 1
            END IF
        END IF
    END WHILE
    WHILE j LESS THAN n DO
        APPEND element AT j of str1 TO answer
        SET j TO j PLUS 1
    END WHILE
    WHILE i LESS THAN m DO
        APPEND element AT i of str2 TO answer
        SET i TO i PLUS 1
    END WHILE
    SET result TO CALL FUNCTION joinCharacters WITH INPUT answer
    RETURN result
END FUNCTION

BEGIN FUNCTION joinCharacters WITH INPUT characters
    SET result TO empty string
    FOR EACH c IN characters DO
        CONCATENATE c TO result
    END FOR
    RETURN result
END FUNCTION

    m = len(pattern)

def build_good_suffix_table(pattern: str) -> list[int]:
    m = len(pattern)
    good_suffix_table = [m] * (m + 1)  # size m+1
    border = [0] * (m + 1)
    i = m
    j = m + 1
    border[i] = j

    for i in range(m + 1):  # iterate to m inclusive
        if good_suffix_table[i] == m:

        if i == j:

    return good_suffix_table

def heapify(h, n):
    for i in range(n // 2, -1, -1):
        k = i
        heap = False
        v = h[k]
        while not heap and 2 * k + 1 < n:
            j = 2 * k + 1  # Left child index
            if j + 1 < n and h[j] < h[j + 1]:  # Right child exists and is greater
                j = j + 1
            if v >= h[j]:  # If the current value is greater or equal to the largest child

def decimal_to_binary(n):
    if n == 0:
        print("0")
        return
    
    binary = []
    while n > 0:
        binary.append(n % 2)  # Append the remainder when dividing by 2
        n = n // 2  # Integer division by 2
    
    binary.reverse()  # Reverse the binary list to get the correct order
    
    # Print the binary number
    print("".join(str(bit) for bit in binary))

        if op in ('+', '-'):
            return 1
        elif op in ('*', '/'):
            return 2
        return 0

    def create_expression_tree(self, infix):
        operands = []  # Stack for operands (numbers or variables)
        operators = []  # Stack for operators

        for current in infix:
            if current == ' ':
                continue
            if current.isalnum():  # If the character is an operand (number or variable)
                operands.append(Node(current))
            elif current == '(':  # If it's an opening parenthesis
                operators.append(current)
            elif current == ')':  # If it's a closing parenthesis
                while operators and operators[-1] != '(':
                    op = operators.pop()
                    right = operands.pop()
                    left = operands.pop()
                    op_node = Node(op)
                    op_node.left = left
                    op_node.right = right
                    operands.append(op_node)
                operators.pop()  # Pop the '('
            elif self.is_operator(current):  # If it's an operator
                while (operators and 
                       self.precedence(operators[-1]) >= self.precedence(current)):
                    op = operators.pop()
                    right = operands.pop()
                    left = operands.pop()
                    op_node = Node(op)

                    op_node.right = right
                    operands.append(op_node)
                operators.append(current)

            op = operators.pop()
            right = operands.pop()
            left = operands.pop()
            op_node = Node(op)
            op_node.left = left
            op_node.right = right
            operands.append(op_node)

        if root:

class Node:
    def __init__(self, value):
        self.value = value  # Node value
        self.left = None     # Left child
        self.right = None    # Right child

def inorder(root):
    if root is not None:
        inorder(root.left)
        print(root.value, end=" ")
        inorder(root.right)

    if root is not None:
        print(root.value, end=" ")
        preorder(root.left)
        preorder(root.right)

def postorder(root):
    if root is not None:
        postorder(root.left)
        postorder(root.right)
        print(root.value, end=" ")

def level_order(root):
    if root is None:
        return
    q = deque([root])  # Queue for level order traversal
    while q:
        current = q.popleft()
        print(current.value, end=" ")
        if current.left:
            q.append(current.left)
        if current.right:
            q.append(current.right)

    def integerBreak(self, n: int) -> int:
        memo = {1: 0, 2: 1}
        return self.count(n, memo)
    
    def count(self, n: int, memo: dict) -> int:
        if n in memo:
            return memo[n]
        
        value = 1
        for i in range(2, n):
            value = max(value, max(i * (n - i), i * self.count(n - i, memo)))
        
        memo[n] = value
        return memo[n]

<s>[INST]Code:class Solution:
    def integerReplacement(self, n: int) -> int:
        cache = {1: 0}
        return self.helper(n, cache)
    
    def helper(self, n: int, cache: dict) -> int:
        if n in cache:
            return cache[n]
        
        if n % 2 == 0:
            cache[n] = 1 + self.helper(n // 2, cache)
        else:
            cache[n] = 1 + min(self.helper(n + 1, cache), self.helper(n - 1, cache))
        
        return cache[n]

    def mini(self, x: int, y: int) -> int:
        return min(x, y)

class Solution:
    def longestPalindrome(self, s: str) -> str:
        start = 0
        size = 0
        
        for i in range(len(s)):
            left = i - 1
            right = i + 1
            cur = 1
            
            # Expand to the right while the characters match
            while right < len(s) and s[i] == s[right]:
                right += 1
                cur += 1
            
            # Expand both sides as long as the characters match
            while left >= 0 and right < len(s):
                if s[left] == s[right]:
                    left -= 1
                    right += 1
                    cur += 2
                else:
                    break
            
            # Adjust left and right to get the actual boundaries of the palindrome
            left += 1
            right -= 1
            
            # Update the longest palindrome found so far
            if cur > size:
                start = left
                size = cur
        
        # Return the substring corresponding to the longest palindrome
        return s[start:start+size]

class Solution:
    def minimumTime(self, grid):
        if grid[0][1] > 1 and grid[1][0] > 1:
            return -1
        rows, cols = len(grid), len(grid[0])
        directions = [(1, 0), (-1, 0), (0, 1), (0, -1)]
        visited = [[False] * cols for _ in range(rows)]
        pq = [(grid[0][0], 0, 0)]
        while pq:
            time, row, col = heapq.heappop(pq)
            if row == rows - 1 and col == cols - 1:
                return time
            if visited[row][col]:
                continue
            visited[row][col] = True
            for d in directions:
                nextRow, nextCol = row + d[0], col + d[1]
                if not self.isValid(visited, nextRow, nextCol):
                    continue
                waitTime = 1 if (grid[nextRow][nextCol] - time) % 2 == 0 else 0
                nextTime = max(grid[nextRow][nextCol] + waitTime, time + 1)
                heapq.heappush(pq, (nextTime, nextRow, nextCol))
        return -1

    def isValid(self, visited, row, col):
        return 0 <= row < len(visited) and 0 <= col < len(visited[0]) and not visited[row][col]

BEGIN FUNCTION minimumTime WITH INPUT grid
    IF element AT 1 IN element AT 0 IN grid GREATER THAN 1 AND element AT 0 IN element AT 1 IN grid GREATER THAN 1
        RETURN MINUS 1
    END IF
    SET rows TO length of grid
    SET cols TO length of element AT 0 IN grid
    INITIALIZE directions AS collection containing pair 1 and 0, pair MINUS 1 and 0, pair 0 and 1, pair 0 and MINUS 1
    INITIALIZE visited AS two dimensional collection of size rows by cols filled with FALSE
    INITIALIZE pq AS priority queue containing tuple of element AT 0 IN element AT 0 IN grid, 0, 0
    WHILE pq IS NOT EQUAL TO EMPTY DO
        SET current TO remove minimum element from pq
        SET time TO first value in current
        SET row TO second value in current
        SET col TO third value in current
        IF row EQUALS rows MINUS 1 AND col EQUALS cols MINUS 1
            RETURN time
        END IF
        IF element AT col IN element AT row IN visited IS TRUE
        ELSE
            SET element AT col IN element AT row IN visited TO TRUE
            FOR EACH d IN directions DO
                SET nextRow TO row PLUS first value in d
                SET nextCol TO col PLUS second value in d
                SET valid TO CALL FUNCTION isValid WITH INPUT visited, nextRow, nextCol
                IF valid IS FALSE
                ELSE
                    IF (element AT nextCol IN element AT nextRow IN grid MINUS time) MODULO 2 EQUALS 0
                        SET waitTime TO 1
                    ELSE
                        SET waitTime TO 0
                    END IF
                    SET nextTime TO MAXIMUM OF element AT nextCol IN element AT nextRow IN grid PLUS waitTime, time PLUS 1
                    add tuple of nextTime, nextRow, nextCol to pq
                END IF
            END FOR
        END IF
    END WHILE
    RETURN MINUS 1
END FUNCTION
BEGIN FUNCTION isValid WITH INPUT visited, row, col

    END IF

        current = self.root

    def _find_node(self, word: str) -> TrieNode:

class SuffixArray:
    def __init__(self, s):
        self.text = s
        self.n = len(s)
        self.suffix_arr = []
        self.lcp = []
        self._build_suffix_array()

    def _counting_sort(self, p, c):
        n = len(p)
        count = [0] * n
        new_p = [0] * n

        for x in c:
            count[x] += 1
        
        pos = [0] * n
        for i in range(1, n):
            pos[i] = pos[i-1] + count[i-1]
        
        for x in p:
            i = c[x]
            new_p[pos[i]] = x
            pos[i] += 1
        
        return new_p

    def _build_suffix_array(self):
        n = self.n
        a = [(self.text[i], i) for i in range(n)]
        a.sort()

        p = [x[1] for x in a]
        c = [0] * n
        c[p[0]] = 0

        for i in range(1, n):
            c[p[i]] = c[p[i-1]] + (a[i][0] != a[i-1][0])

        k = 0
        while (1 << k) < n:
            for i in range(n):
                p[i] = (p[i] - (1 << k) + n) % n
            
            p = self._counting_sort(p, c)

            new_c = [0] * n
            new_c[p[0]] = 0
            for i in range(1, n):
                prev = (c[p[i-1]], c[(p[i-1] + (1 << k)) % n])
                curr = (c[p[i]], c[(p[i] + (1 << k)) % n])
                new_c[p[i]] = new_c[p[i-1]] + (prev != curr)
            
            c = new_c
            k += 1

        self.suffix_arr = p
        self._compute_lcp()

    def _compute_lcp(self):
        n = self.n
        rank = [0] * n
        for i in range(n):
            rank[self.suffix_arr[i]] = i

        self.lcp = [0] * n
        k = 0
        for i in range(n):
            if rank[i] == n - 1:
                k = 0
                continue
            
            j = self.suffix_arr[rank[i] + 1]
            while i + k < n and j + k < n and self.text[i + k] == self.text[j + k]:
                k += 1
            
            self.lcp[rank[i]] = k
            k = max(k - 1, 0)

    def get_suffix_array(self):
        return self.suffix_arr

    def get_lcp(self):
        return self.lcp

BEGIN FUNCTION InitializeSuffixArray WITH INPUT s
    SET text TO s
    SET n TO LENGTH OF s
    INITIALIZE suffix_arr AS EMPTY list
    INITIALIZE lcp AS EMPTY list
    CALL FUNCTION BuildSuffixArray WITH INPUT text, n, suffix_arr, lcp

BEGIN FUNCTION CountingSort WITH INPUT p, c
    SET n TO LENGTH OF p
    INITIALIZE count AS list of length n filled with 0
    INITIALIZE new_p AS list of length n filled with 0
    FOR EACH x IN c DO
        SET count AT x TO count AT x PLUS 1
    END FOR
    INITIALIZE pos AS list of length n filled with 0
    INITIALIZE i AS 1
    WHILE i LESS THAN n DO
        SET pos AT i TO pos AT (i MINUS 1) PLUS count AT (i MINUS 1)
        SET i TO i PLUS 1
    END WHILE
    FOR EACH x IN p DO
        SET i TO c AT x
        SET new_p AT (pos AT i) TO x
        SET pos AT i TO pos AT i PLUS 1
    END FOR
    RETURN new_p
END FUNCTION

BEGIN FUNCTION BuildSuffixArray WITH INPUT text, n, suffix_arr, lcp
